step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the decision to select Option B ("Different individuals but of same age groups"). This selection best captures the essential feature of cross-sectional studies — examining a group of people who share similar characteristics such as being part of the same age cohort, thereby ensuring comparability and reducing variability due to differences in life stages or historical contexts.

raw_response:
I agree with the decision to select Option B ("Different individuals but of same age groups"). This selection best captures the essential feature of cross-sectional studies — examining a group of people who share similar characteristics such as being part of the same age cohort, thereby ensuring comparability and reducing variability due to differences in life stages or historical contexts. 

The focus on contemporaneous data collection remains crucial because it isolates specific conditions or exposures pertinent only to those living through certain times rather than attributing changes over longer periods incorrectly. Therefore, my agreement stands firm with the selected answer: **Option B**.
